# Viral Video Research Pack

This pack turns the chat research into a structured working system for short-form video optimisation across TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Instagram Reels.

## What this research is

This is **secondary research + strategic synthesis**. It combines:

- official platform documentation from TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, and Meta;
- academic research on virality, captions, text overlays, storytelling, face presence, attention, cognitive load, diffusion, and behaviour design;
- grey-source benchmarks from Metricool, Socialinsider, Buffer, Hootsuite, and similar industry research;
- a large hypothesis bank and experiment backlog for primary testing.

## What this research is not yet

This is **not yet primary evidence from your own channels**. We have not yet:

- analysed your actual TikTok/Reels/Shorts analytics;
- run controlled A/B tests on your content;
- scraped or coded 100–300 niche videos into a creative genome database;
- statistically validated which mechanisms work for your niche and audience.

## Main conclusion

The useful frame is not “hack the algorithm.” The working model is:

```text
Algorithmic distribution = observable viewer behaviour
Viewer behaviour = attention + comprehension + emotion + social value + trust
Creative tactics = tools that move one behaviour at a time
```

The strongest tactical doctrine:

```text
First frame earns the view.
Hook earns the next 3 seconds.
First payoff earns the body.
Proof earns trust.
Structure earns completion.
Utility earns saves.
Identity earns sends.
Prompt earns comments.
Series earns follows.
Search alignment earns long tail.
```
